T0062.title: Online Lending
T0062.definition: Refers to small loans issued through internet platforms, which are frequently exploited by fraud rings for fraudulent applications.
T0062.description: Fraud rings use synthetic or stolen identities to register for and apply to various online lending products in bulk. They typically target small, cash-advance platforms with weak risk controls, maliciously cashing out through a practice known as 'loan farming' and then disappearing after the loan is disbursed. This behavior leads to massive bad debts for online lending platforms and is a contributing factor to the violent debt collection chaos.
T0062.usageExample: He created a bunch of fake profiles to specifically target online loan products that don't check credit history. Any money he got was considered a 'payday,' and he never intended to pay it back.
